quiz1.missionText: Archive the 3rd email from the top

insight.title: How did you know swiping would archive?
insight.subtitle: Direction and icons teach the action
insight.swipeDesc: In mail and task apps, swiping right-to-left is a standard UI pattern meaning 'archive' or 'complete'. Gmail also uses this pattern.
insight.greenDesc: While red means 'delete/danger', green is used in UIs worldwide to mean 'save/safe'.
insight.archiveDesc: A box with a downward arrow (📦) is the common archive icon across Gmail and Outlook. Its meaning is understood without reading any language.

insight.title: How did you navigate to trash via the drawer?
insight.subtitle: The hamburger menu is the folder gateway
insight.drawerDesc: The three-line (☰) icon is a universal UI symbol meaning 'open hidden menu'. Gmail and Outlook use the same icon.
insight.trashTitle: Trash icon = home for deleted items
insight.trashDesc: The 🗑️ icon indicates 'trash/deleted items' regardless of OS. Called Trash in Gmail and 'Deleted' in iOS, but the icon instantly identifies the location.
insight.emptyDesc: Trash is a temporary storage area. The design that data remains until you press Empty Trash is a safety design to prevent accidental deletion.

insight.title: How did you know long press enables multi-select?
insight.subtitle: Long press is the entry to selection mode
insight.longPressTitle: Long press = switch to selection mode
insight.longPressDesc: In mail and file management apps, long press is the standard interaction to start multi-select mode. Gmail and iOS Mail use the same pattern.
insight.checkDesc: The ✓ mark is a universal symbol for 'selected'. Combined with checkboxes, you can see how many items are selected at a glance.
insight.headerDesc: Entering selection mode turns the header blue and shows count and action icons. This 'context-dependent UI change' is the standard Android Contextual Action Bar pattern.

quiz4.hint: Tip: Use larger:5M to search by size!

insight.title: How did you think of the 'larger:5M' command?
insight.subtitle: Search operators narrow down emails
insight.operatorTitle: Search operators = specify conditions with commands
insight.operatorDesc: Gmail offers advanced filtering with search operators like 'larger:5M', 'from:alice', 'has:attachment'. This is not just for programmers - it's a feature for all users.
insight.sizeDesc: 'M' stands for megabytes (MB). This search condition is used to find emails with large attachments - a standard Gmail feature for years.
insight.hintTitle: Hint feature guides to the answer
insight.hintDesc: The '?' icon or 'Hint' button acts as a lifeline in many apps. Even if you don't know search commands, the hint design guides you to the answer.
